title: BABIES FIRST! - 211info
text: LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH ALBANY BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 1050 SW 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 967-3888 2489 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 967-3888 Main phone (541) 926-2102 FAX (800) 735-1232 TTY/TTD (800) 304-7468 Toll-free Main phone (541) 967-3888 ext 2489 Laura Jimenez [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to register FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Linn County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Services: Case/Care Management * Families with Infants/Toddlers Home Based Parenting Education Nutrition Education * Families with Infants/Toddlers Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs * At Risk Families Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CONNECTS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - NURSE FAMILY PARTNERSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
